raw_response:
Based on the Q4 2025 evidence reviewed, Atlas is **not fully ready for CC6.4**. The physical security program has several effective elements, but there are gaps in colocation oversight, access review evidence, restricted-zone visitor approval, and environmental control maintenance.

| # | Finding | Related Document / Section | Why It’s a Gap | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Colocation annual physical security audit appears overdue for Q4 2025** | Colocation Audit Report, Sections 1, 7; Physical Security Policy, Section 8.2 | The only colocation physical security audit provided was performed on **October 18, 2024**, with the next audit due by **October 18, 2025**. For the Q4 2025 audit period, no 2025 annual audit was provided. Because production infrastructure is hosted at NorthStar, Atlas needs current evidence that physical security controls, Atlas cabinet access, CCTV coverage, and provider controls remain effective. | **High** |
| 2 | **2025 colocation cabinet lock rotation and access-list review not evidenced** | Colocation Audit Report, Sections 3.3 and 7; Physical Security Policy, Sections 8.2 and 8.3 | The 2024 audit states cabinet lock rotation was last performed **August 1, 2024**, with the next rotation due **February 2025**. No evidence was provided showing the February 2025 or later semiannual lock rotations occurred. Also, the policy requires quarterly review of the NorthStar cabinet access list, but no Q4 2025 or 2025 access-list review evidence was provided. | **Medium** |
| 3 | **Quarterly Atlas badge access review not evidenced** | Physical Security Policy, Section 4.4; Badge Access Log Q4 2025 | The policy requires the Security Manager to review all active badges quarterly within 15 business days of quarter end and resolve exceptions within 5 business days. The evidence provided is an activity log, but not an access review showing the active badge population, reviewer, date performed, appropriateness of access, exceptions, or remediation. The Q3 review would have been due during Q4 2025, but no review evidence was provided. | **Medium** |
| 4 | **Badge access log completeness is questionable for all Atlas-controlled access points** | Physical Security Policy, Section 3.2; Badge Access Log Q4 2025; Visitor Log Q4 2025 | The Q4 badge log is described as covering all Atlas-controlled access points, but it only shows activity for **AP-SUITE-01** and **AP-SRV-01**. The policy lists additional controlled points, including **AP-NET-01**, **AP-EXEC-01**, and **AP-BACK-01**. The visitor log shows access to the **Network Closet** and **Executive Area** during Q4, but no corresponding AP-NET-01 or AP-EXEC-01 badge events appear. This raises concern that logs are incomplete, certain doors are not logging, or access controls are not operating as documented. | **Medium** |
| 5 | **Restricted-zone visitor access occurred without documented required approval** | Visitor Log Q4 2025, VIS-0415; Physical Security Policy, Section 6.4 | Visitor **VIS-0415** accessed the **Server Room**, which is a restricted zone, but the log states **“N/A”** for restricted-zone approval and notes that no Infrastructure Director pre-approval was documented. Policy requires written pre-approval from the Infrastructure Director for visitor access to restricted zones. | **Medium** |
| 6 | **Approval authority for some restricted-zone visits is inconsistent with policy** | Visitor Log Q4 2025, Executive Area visits; Physical Security Policy, Section 6.4 | Several Executive Area visits were approved by the CISO rather than the Infrastructure Director. While this may be reasonable operationally, the policy specifically requires written pre-approval from the Infrastructure Director for restricted-zone visitor access. Atlas should either follow the stated approval authority or update the policy to reflect acceptable delegates or alternate approvers. | **Low** |
| 7 | **Environmental sensor calibration overdue for server room sensors** | Environmental Monitoring Log Q4 2025; Physical Security Policy, Section 9.3 | Sensors **TEMP-02** and **HUM-02** were last calibrated on **April 15, 2025**, with calibration due **October 15, 2025**. They were **77 days overdue as of December 31, 2025**. Although readings remained within normal ranges and redundant sensors were current, overdue calibration reduces confidence in the accuracy of environmental monitoring for protected assets. | **Medium** |
| 8 | **Environmental alert thresholds do not fully align with stated operating standards** | Physical Security Policy, Sections 9.1 and 9.2 | The policy states server room standards of **64–75°F** and **40–60% humidity**, but alert thresholds are configured at **>77°F** and **>55% humidity**. This creates a design inconsistency: temperature could exceed the stated acceptable range without generating an alert. Atlas should align alerting thresholds with the defined environmental standards or document the rationale for the variance. | **Low** |
| 9 | **Fire suppression inspection outcome not evidenced** | Visitor Log Q4 2025, VIS-0403; Physical Security Policy, Section 9.4 | The visitor log shows FireSafe Systems visited for an FM-200 annual inspection, but no inspection report, test result, pass/fail status, or remediation record was provided. A visitor entry alone does not evidence that the fire suppression system was tested successfully per policy. | **Low** |
| 10 | **Shared building access risks are acknowledged but compensating-control evidence is limited** | Lease Agreement, Sections 12.1, 12.4, 13.1; Physical Security Policy, Sections 3.3–3.5 | The building lobby is open during business hours, and stairwells are unlocked due to fire code, allowing individuals in the building to reach tenant floors without Atlas-controlled authentication. Atlas relies primarily on the suite entrance badge reader and hallway CCTV as compensating controls. This may be acceptable, but the evidence does not show risk acceptance, periodic review of the compensating controls, or testing of CAM-01 monitoring effectiveness. | **Low** |
